title: Earnings Call Transcript: Robinhood Q1 2026 Misses Forecasts, Stock Dips
text: Robinhood Markets reported Q1 2026 earnings results that fell short of Wall Street expectations, triggering a significant market selloff. Q1 2026 Results Summary: Total net revenues increased 15% year-over-year to $1.07 billion, missing expectations of $1.14 billion. Diluted EPS was $0.38, missing the expected $0.39. The results marked a meaningful deceleration from the company's recent growth trajectory. Key Headwinds: Cryptocurrency revenue declined 47% year-over-year to $134 million, reflecting a broader crypto market slowdown. Transaction-based revenues grew 7% year-over-year to $623 million. Positive Drivers: Event contracts surged 320% year-over-year, demonstrating success in prediction markets. Equities revenue grew 46% year-over-year. Net Deposits: Continued strength with $18 billion at a 22% annualized growth rate. Gold Subscribers: Reached a record 4.3 million, up 36% year-over-year. Market Reaction: Shares fell 13% in the immediate aftermath, with year-to-date decline of 37%. The selloff reflected broader fintech sector weakness and concerns about trading volume sustainability. Positive Factors: The SEC's elimination of the Pattern Day Trader rule on April 14 was expected to boost future results when it takes effect June 4. Analyst Outlook: While sentiment turned negative near-term, the analyst consensus remained constructive with 36 buy ratings and only 3 hold ratings.
